---
title: "Exploratory Analysis"
output: pdf_document
date: "2023-12-05"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
#set up libraries
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library('naivebayes')
library('C50')


#Open file
# Set your working directory to the folder containing NBAPlayerData.csv
# setwd("path/to/your/data")
nbadf <- read.csv('NBAPlayerData.csv')
```


#statistical summary of data
```{r}
summary(nbadf)
```


##visualization plot #1: scatterplot for points per game on x axis and Minutes played
##on the y axis. Colored by team
```{r}
ggplot(data = nbadf)+
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = PTS , y = MP, color = W.L.), size = 1) +
  labs(title = "NBA Player Points vs Minutes Played",
       x = "Points (PTS)",
       y = "Minutes Played (MP)")
```

##visualization plot #2: Density plot of minutes played per team
```{r}
ggplot(nbadf, aes(x = MP, fill = Tm)) +
  geom_density(aes(y = ..density..)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Tm) +
  labs(title = "Density Plot of Minutes Played by Team", x = "Minutes Played", y = "Density")
  
```

##visualization plot #3: Histogram showing the distribution of minutes played throughout the league
```{r}
ggplot(nbadf, aes(x = MP)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "blue", color = "black") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Minutes Played", x = "Minutes Played", y = "Frequency")
```

#statistical test #1: 
```{r}
teamA <- nbadf[nbadf$Tm  %in% c("LAL"), ]$MP
teamB <- nbadf[nbadf$Tm %in% c("WAS"), ]$MP
t.test(teamA, teamB)
```


#statistical test #2:
```{r}
nbadf$GB <- as.factor(nbadf$GB)
nbadf$W.L. <- as.factor(nbadf$W.L.)

# Perform two-way ANOVA
result_anova <- aov(MP ~ GB + W.L., data = nbadf)
print(result_anova)
```
